channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Proposed Bill Could Force Shutdown of Illegal Football Streaming in Kenya
item.description: Kenyans who love watching football through illegal streaming sites may soon have to change how they follow their favourite teams. A new bill, the Copyright and Related Rights Bill, seeks to require In...

item.title: Kim Kardashian stuns in gold gown at Vanity Fair Oscars party despite challenging heels
item.description: Kim Kardashian attended the Vanity Fair Oscars party in Los Angeles wearing a striking skintight golden gown by Gucci. The 45-year-old reality star paired the ensemble with extremely high mirrored gol...

item.title: Ambassador Mahmoud Kombo joins EALA as ex officio member
item.description: Dar es Salaam – The Minister for Foreign Affairs and East African Cooperation, Mahmoud Thabit Kombo, has officially become a Member of the East African Legislative Assembly (EALA). The ceremony took p...

item.title: Sara Duterte files answer to impeachment complaints, challenges House procedure
item.description: Vice President Sara Duterte submitted her verified answer to two impeachment complaints filed against her on March 16, 2026, the final day to respond. Instead of addressing the allegations directly, D...

item.title: Andhra Pradesh forest officials plan to safely tranquillise tiger near Annavaram temple
item.description: The Andhra Pradesh State Forest Department, in coordination with an expert committee formed by the National Tiger Conservation Authority (NTCA), has initiated operations to tranquillise a male tiger n...

item.title: Drone strike on fuel tank disrupts flights at Dubai Airport, affecting thousands of UK travellers
item.description: Dubai International Airport experienced a major disruption after a drone struck a nearby fuel tank, leading to the suspension of departures and arrivals. The incident, which occurred in the early hour...

item.title: Court Considers Legality of Presidential Election Inquiry Commission
item.description: Dar es Salaam. Lawyers representing citizens who challenged the establishment of the Presidential Commission of Inquiry into the October 29, 2025 General Election have presented several legal objectio...

item.title: North West Reports 179 Confirmed Foot and Mouth Disease Cases, Vaccination Efforts Intensify
item.description: The North West Department of Agriculture and Rural Development has reported a rise in confirmed Foot and Mouth Disease (FMD) cases in the province, now totaling 179 as of 6 March 2026. The Dr Kenneth ...

item.title: Hamas Meets Trump-Led Board Amid Gaza Tensions During Iran Conflict
item.description: Envoys from US President Donald Trump’s newly formed 'Board of Peace' recently held talks with Hamas representatives in Cairo, aiming to maintain the fragile Gaza ceasefire. These discussions come aft...

item.title: Remo Stars Captain Talks Win and Survival Battle in NPFL
item.description: Remo Stars captain Victor Mbaoma don talk about di important 2–1 victory wey dem get against Nasarawa United for their home stadium in Ikenne. Dis win na big relief for di team because e put dem well ...

item.title: Australian Government Introduces Stricter Requirements for Migration Agents
item.description: The Australian government, under Labor, is introducing stricter regulations for migration agents to boost integrity in the visa application system. From April, registered agents will need to complete ...

item.title: Bristol nursery worker Nathan Bennett sentenced to 30 years for raping and sexually assaulting five toddlers
item.description: A 30-year-old man named Nathan Bennett has been sentenced to 30 years in prison, with at least 24 years in custody followed by six years on licence, after being convicted of serious sexual offences ag...

item.title: Tracy Ohiri Explains Reason Behind Apology To David Umahi
item.description: Tracy Ohiri don finally talk publicly about wetin make her apologize to David Umahi. Inside the interview, she yan say she wan settle any misunderstanding wey fit dey between dem and make peace prevai...

item.title: Criminal Convictions of the Alexander Brothers Highlight Political and Economic Connections in the U.S.
item.description: A Manhattan court has sentenced Tal, Oren, and Alon Alexander for charges including pedophilia and rape. The brothers allegedly drugged and sexually assaulted multiple victims across borders and were ...

item.title: Intelligence Bureau to oversee implementation of national austerity measures in government offices
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has directed the Intelligence Bureau (IB) to monitor the enforcement of the national austerity plan across all government institutions. According to an official notificat...

item.title: Philippine Embassy Confirms Arrest of Two OFWs in UAE Amid Rising Regional Tensions
item.description: The Philippine embassy in the United Arab Emirates confirmed on March 16, 2026, that two overseas Filipino workers (OFWs) were arrested by local authorities. While the embassy has verified their ident...

item.title: U.S. Allies Hesitant to Support Trump’s Efforts in the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: U.S. allies have expressed reluctance to participate in President Donald Trump's proposed mission to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, which has been effectively closed following U.S. and Israeli attacks o...

item.title: Kaspersky Reports Sharp Rise in Spyware and Password Stealer Attacks in Nigeria During 2025
item.description: Kaspersky don release report wey show say for 2025, dia security tools block more than 4 million online attack attempts on Nigerian users, plus another 9 million on-device threats wey include malware ...

item.title: Mental health laws in Africa often overlook traditional and community care
item.description: For many people in Africa, when mental health problems show up, they first go to traditional healers, prayer camps, or community elders rather than hospitals. The story of Idoko, a young man from rura...

item.title: Iranian women's football players reverse asylum claims in Australia amid reported threats and family pressure
item.description: Several members of the Iranian women's national football team, who were competing in the Women's Asian Cup in Australia, initially sought asylum after refusing to sing their national anthem before a m...
